series.title: [PATCH] ui/dbus: associate add_client completion with its request

review.summary: The patch correctly fixes the async request association bug in `ui/dbus.c` by binding completion state to the originating request, holding the necessary references for callback lifetime safety, and ignoring stale completions after replacement requests. I did not identify a functional regression in the proposed change.

stages.A: Problem statement is valid: the previous callback used global state and could act on a newer request than the one that initiated the async operation.
stages.B: Lifetime handling is improved: the callback now owns references to both `DBusDisplay` and the request `GCancellable`, avoiding use-after-free of callback context.
stages.C: Request matching is implemented correctly: pointer equality against the referenced cancellable is sufficient here because the callback holds its own reference, preventing reuse of the same object address.
stages.D: Cancellation/replacement flow is sound: an existing request is cancelled and cleared before issuing a new one, and stale completions no longer clear or install state for newer requests.
stages.E: Error handling is improved appropriately: cancelled operations are no longer reported as failures, while real setup failures still surface via `error_printf()`. No additional blocking issues found from the patch itself.

disclaimer: LLM-generated draft. Not an authoritative review.
